cases.title: Balanced pre-merge review with visible evidence
cases.user_request: Review this open PR for merge risk and tell me what, if anything, still needs attention before I merge it.

notes: The PR diff, title, and CI status are available.
The response should stay pre-merge and focus on engineering risk.

static_checks: Keeps the review focused on merge risk rather than post-merge impact.
review_questions: Does the response identify concrete PR surfaces rather than generic code review themes?
Does it distinguish merge risk from normal maintainability commentary?
Would the follow-up actions be small enough to execute before merge?

cases.title: Behavior change with weak or missing validation
cases.user_request: Is this PR safe to merge? I want the risk review to focus on missing tests or validation gaps.

notes: The PR changes behavior in a visible way.
Validation signals are incomplete or do not cover the changed path well.

static_checks: Treats missing validation as a risk signal when the diff changes behavior.
review_questions: Does the response explain why the validation is insufficient for the changed behavior?
Does it avoid turning a weak suspicion into a firm bug claim?
Does it recommend a targeted test or check instead of a broad rewrite?

cases.title: Migration, flag, or rollback risk needs explicit handling
cases.user_request: Review the PR for rollout risk, especially migrations, feature flags, compatibility, and rollback safety.

notes: The PR touches a rollout-sensitive surface such as a migration, config, or feature flag.
The response should call out missing ordering or fallback assumptions if they matter.

static_checks: Calls out rollout and rollback gaps instead of treating the PR as safe by default.
review_questions: Does the response identify the rollout-sensitive surface clearly?
Does it mention ordering, compatibility, or rollback concerns where relevant?
Would the recommendation help the author make the PR safer without broadening scope?

cases.title: Use local preferences to raise caution on sensitive PR surfaces
cases.user_request: Review this PR for merge risk and keep the follow-up actions small enough to finish before merge.

notes: The PR touches a migration file, so the response should raise caution and may include a fuller explanation or alternate next action, but it must stay pre-merge and evidence-led.

static_checks: Uses the local preference file to raise caution without turning it into a source of speculative risk.
review_questions: Does the response use the sensitive-path preference to inspect the migration surface carefully without inventing unsupported findings?
Does it keep the recommendation small and pre-merge even if the report becomes more detailed?
Does it avoid treating `.ai/swe.json` as stronger than the visible PR evidence?
